comment: Launcher manifest (issue #226, scope item 2): the curated list of the bundled Toys. Each entry's `id` is the instrument document name under instruments/<id>.json — and the id the player auto-discovers a surface doc by (surfaces/<id>.web.json ?? surfaces/<id>.json ?? auto-derived from the interface pipes, ADR-0043; no manifest field needed); `title`/`blurb` are player-facing (blurbs adapted from the README rig table); `kind` is self-playing (sounds on load), tap-to-play (surfaces trigger widgets), or live-input (takes the mic — the player renders an Enable-microphone control, issue #248); `order` sets grid position (also the cold-start gallery's ranking, spec §2.2 — show-all, no auto-derivation). `chips` (issue #357, spec §2.3/§I): an OPTIONAL array of 2-3 sensory strings, authored in `plain` register (spec §8.4), shown as tappable quick-change chips on the gallery-pick's proactive turn one. A chip posts VERBATIM as the user's chat turn — no hidden label/prompt pair. Absent/empty means a greeting-only turn one (tailored-or-nothing, no generic filler); it does NOT gate a Toy's presence in the gallery. The staging script (scripts/stage-assets.mjs) stages exactly these Toys + their transitive resources, plus every surface doc, into public/. Default Toy is groovebox (order 1) — its assets are prefetched at splash so the first pick is instant.

toys.title: Groovebox
toys.blurb: A free-running 16-step drum machine — kick, snare, and hat synthesized from operators on a shared clock. Toggle steps, ride the tempo.

toys.title: Chord Player
toys.blurb: Tap and hold diatonic triad buttons (I–vii°). Chords re-spell live when you change key — a 12-voice pad.

toys.title: Strum Harp
toys.blurb: Drag to strum — the bar plucks a note each time it crosses a string. Strings stay in key through the tonal context.

toys.title: Euclidean Drums
toys.blurb: A self-playing drum machine with knobs to change beat density.

toys.title: Mic Space
toys.blurb: Your microphone through a tunable reverb — speak, sing, or play into it and hear yourself in a big room. Headphones required.
